summary: AI timekeeping and task-intelligence platform for law firms.
editor_note: The useful bet is that timekeeping should be inferred from work traces rather than reconstructed after the fact. Billables AI shows Microsoft 365, Teams, Adobe, Chrome, Edge, Zoom, and practice-platform integrations plus matter matching and narrative generation. Unlike passive timers, it focuses on task intelligence. The risk is lawyer adoption when privileged work and billing review meet AI suggestions.

sources.label: Billables AI Series A funding announcement

product_evidence.claim: The official site positions Billables AI as automated timekeeping and task intelligence for lawyers, consultants, and professional services.
Billables AI describes workflow tracking, AI matter matching, narrative generation, and daily customized billable activity reports.
The product surface lists integrations with Microsoft 365, Teams, Adobe, Chrome, Edge, Zoom, and practice-management platforms.
The careers page confirms an official hiring surface without requiring VentureDex to scrape roles, locations, or job counts.

market_context.primary_user: Lawyers, consultants, finance teams, and professional-services operators responsible for accurate billable-time capture.
market_context.differentiation: Billables AI infers time and task context from work activity rather than relying on manual timers or after-the-fact reconstruction.
market_context.why_now: Professional-services firms are trying to reduce revenue leakage while maintaining review control over client, matter, and narrative entries.

risks.claim: The open question is how firms balance automated time suggestions with privileged context, lawyer review habits, and client billing trust.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
